source_jobs.title: Senior Characterization & Reliability Engineer for Power Semiconductors
source_jobs.description:
Company Description

Vertical Semiconductor, a transformative MIT spin-out, is leading the development of vertical gallium nitride (GaN) power transistors which will revolutionize power electronics in industries such as data centers, energy systems, renewable energy and electric vehicles (EVs).



Role Description

As a rapidly growing startup, we are seeking a full-time Senior Characterization & Reliability Engineer to help drive the commercialization of vertical GaN technology and support our engineering team through its critical stages of growth. This role will focus on characterizing vertical GaN devices for a variety of target applications using both on-wafer and packaged device test setups. You will have opportunity to be hands-on in shaping the development of a world-changing power electronics, and your contributions will directly impact the success of the company and the industries we aim to disrupt. We have a preference for candidates who are able to work from our office at Greentown Labs (Somerville, MA), but are open to considering hybrid candidates.



Key Skills

 - Deep knowledge of electrical characterization of power devices (IV, CV, pulsed IV, gate charge, etc.) using high-voltage test instrumentation and curve tracers for on-wafer probing with semiautomatic probe stations.

 - Ability to design circuit boards for packaged device tests such as UIS and switching performance.

 - Familiarity with JEDEC qualification standards (HTRB, HTGB, TDDB, thermal cycling, etc.) and their evaluation.

 - Strong computer skills with proficiency in python/MATLAB or similar for data analysis and measurement automation.

 - Well-versed in key power device parameter extraction for data sheets.

 - Experience with schematic entry, circuit simulation, and PCB layout tools.

 - Desire to work in a highly-collaborative, fast-paced startup environment.

 - Power circuit design experience with GaN or SiC preferred.

 - Excellent communication skills, with the ability to work effectively across cross-functional teams and with external partners.



Qualifications

Applicants should have a master’s degree or PhD in electrical engineering with a focus on power device design/characterization or have 5+ years of relevant industry experience. Familiarity with power devices for medium voltage (600-1200 V) applications such as GaN HEMTs, SiC MOSFETs, or Si IGBTs is strongly preferred. Applicant should be comfortable working closely with semiconductor device engineers and expect a high degree of ownership in their role.



Compensation

We offer a competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package along with participation in our stock option program that is commensurable with candidates’ experience and impact.



Values

Vertical Semiconductor is seeking an engineer who is not only capable of accelerating the technical vision of the company but is also willing to get involved in the day-to-day execution of a startup. The ideal candidate is someone who enjoys tackling complex engineering challenges, is comfortable with a hands-on approach in a collaborative arena and thrives in a startup environment where they can make an immediate impact. We value integrity, initiative, problem-solving, creativity and a passion for creating transformative technology that can change industries and improve the world.

source_jobs.title: Principal Semiconductor Device Engineer
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION

Vertical Semiconductor, a venture-backed MIT spin-out, is leading the development of vertical gallium nitride (GaN) power electronics to build the foundational power layer for the next generation of compute and AI.




ROLE DESCRIPTION

Vertical Semiconductor is seeking a Principal Semiconductor Device Engineer to serve as a senior technical contributor in the development and commercialization of our vertical GaN power device technology. This is a hands-on role is intended for an engineer with around 10-20 years of semiconductor R&D experience who can operate at the intersection of device physics, process integration, and manufacturability.

In this role, you will provide deep technical leadership across device architecture, process development, modeling, and characterization, while working closely with cross-functional partners in epitaxy, process engineering, packaging, reliability, and systems. You will play a critical role in defining device roadmaps, de-risking technology transitions, and guiding decisions that directly impact product performance and scalability.

This position offers the opportunity to help shape a world-changing power electronics platform at a pivotal stage, with direct influence on the company’s technical direction and long-term success. We prefer candidates who can work from our office at Greentown Labs (Somerville, MA).




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as a technical subject-matter expert for vertical GaN and wide-bandgap power devices across the full R&D lifecycle, from concept through early production readiness

 - Lead the definition and evolution of device architectures, performance targets, and design trade-offs for next-generation vertical GaN power devices

 - Drive device modeling, simulation, and analytical understanding to guide design optimization, scaling strategies, and reliability improvements

 - Plan and execute device development programs in shared fabs and external foundries, including technology transfer and process co-development

 - Partner closely with internal and external teams on CMOS-compatible GaN process integration, yield learning, and manufacturability

 - Provide technical leadership in failure analysis, reliability physics, and root-cause investigations for wide-bandgap power devices

 - Oversee and interpret on-wafer and discrete device characterization, including DC, CV, dynamic, and high-power testing

 - Develop and review GDS layouts, test structures, and evaluation vehicles to support device learning and validation

 - Support system-level understanding through application evaluation boards, measurements, and correlation to device behavior

 - Mentor junior engineers and influence technical best practices through informal technical leadership, design reviews, and knowledge sharing

 - Contribute to IP development, publications, and external technical engagements as appropriate


QUALIFICATIONS

 - PhD or equivalent industry experience in Electrical Engineering, Physics, Materials Science, or Device Engineering

 - Approximately 15–25 years of professional R&D experience in semiconductor device development

 - Deep expertise in GaN or other wide-bandgap semiconductors (e.g., SiC), with a strong preference for power device experience

 - Demonstrated track record of technical impact and innovation in advanced device development programs


REQUIRED TECHNICAL SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

 - Extensive experience with power device design in GaN and/or SiC, including vertical device concepts

 - In-depth understanding of semiconductor manufacturing processes, integration challenges, and yield considerations

 - Proven experience working in shared fab and foundry environments, including external technology transfers

 - Strong proficiency in TCAD simulation for device physics, scaling, and optimization

 - Advanced data analysis skills using Python, MATLAB, or equivalent tools

 - Hands-on experience with GDS layout and test structure design

 - Expertise

source_jobs.title: Principal Applications Engineer
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION

Vertical Semiconductor, a venture-backed MIT spin-out, is leading the development of vertical gallium nitride (GaN) power semiconductors to build the foundational power layer for the next generation of compute and AI.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We’re hiring a Principal Applications Engineer to help commercialize next-generation Vertical GaN power devices (100 V - 2300 V) by translating device performance into system-level wins for customers. This role blends customer-facing technical leadership, hands-on power converter evaluation, simulation/model development, and bench characterization of fast-switching wide bandgap devices. The role offers significant ownership of customer technical engagement, and the development of evaluation hardware, reference designs, and technical content (application notes, trainings, demos). As the company scales, this position has the strong potential to evolve into a team leadership role.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Customer technical leadership / design-in acceleration

 - Serve as senior technical interface for strategic accounts: translate customer specs into power device requirements; support design in activity including simulations, testing, troubleshooting, and performance optimization.

 - Present Vertical Semiconductor technology, products, and roadmap to customers and partners; act as a subject-matter expert for target applications.

Simulation, modeling, and system enablement

 - Develop and own SPICE and/or PLECS simulation workflows for Vertical GaN in realistic converter environments (switching behavior, parasitics, loss/thermal estimation, gate-drive tradeoffs).

 - Build reusable solution templates (topology starting points, loop inductance/parasitic models, measurement-informed parameters) to rapidly evaluate customer use cases.

 - Create system collateral: application block diagrams, design guidelines, technical notes, and simulation models as part of an enablement roadmap.

Evaluation hardware & reference design leadership

 - Lead development of evaluation boards, demo boards, and reference designs (schematic/layout, BOM, bring-up plan, test plan, characterization report).

 - Perform system-level bench testing and debug; drive design optimizations based on measured performance.

High-voltage WBG bench characterization

 - Where needed, perform hands-on characterization of fast-switching WBG devices, including switching waveform integrity, parasitic-sensitive measurements, and robustness characterization.

 - Develop and publish practical “design rules” for customers: gate drive, layout/loop inductance, protection strategies, and measurement techniques.

Technical content and technical marketing

 - Develop application notes, design guides, trainings/webinars, and other media that make Vertical GaN easy to adopt.

Cross-functional product influence

 - Provide structured customer feedback to Device Engineering and Product teams: performance requirements, feature requests, failure-mode learnings, and competitive insights.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years in applications engineering supporting power semiconductor customers OR 5+ years developing power systems/converters.

 - 5+ years experience developing or working with power converters (e.g., inverters, PFC, isolated DC-DC). Experience delivering reference designs or complete converter hardware is a strong plus.

 - Proven bench characterization experience with high voltage and high current wide bandgap devices.

 - Strong understanding of high-frequency power converter design (switching loss mechanisms, magnetics implications, layout/parasitics, gate drive).

 - Deep familiarity applying SiC and GaN devices in real converters (including design-in pitfalls and best practices).

 - Proficiency with SPICE and/or PLECS; ability to develop models and quickly evaluate power circuits.

 - Strong technical communication skills: clear written application guidance and confident in customer-facing engagement.

 - Exper

notes: Vertical Semiconductor, a venture-backed MIT spin-out, is leading the development of vertical gallium nitride (GaN) power semiconductors.
